Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/iqtree.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-dw992cfw...

Analyzing iqtree AUR package (working in /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-dw992cfw) with qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD only sources from a git repository via the declared `source` array entry: `git+$url.git#tag=v${pkgver}`, where `url` points to the official GitHub repository for iqtree3. This is a standard and expected source retrieval method in AUR packages. The `md5sums` array includes a fixed checksum for the v3.1.3 tag, which helps ensure source integrity.

No command substitutions, unescaped interpolations, or executable snippets occur in the global scope or within `pkgver()`, which could trigger unintended code execution when sourcing the PKGBUILD. The `pkgver()` function is absent, so version resolution depends solely on the static `pkgver` variable. All operations during sourcing—parsing variables and resolving sources—are safe and aligned with standard PKGBUILD practices.

The `prepare()`, `build()`, and `package()` functions contain typical build steps (git submodules, cmake, make, install), but these are not executed under `--nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`. Thus, they are out of scope for this safety gate.
</details>
<summary>
Safe source retrieval, no malicious code on sourcing.</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- Safe source retrieval, no malicious code on sourcing.[/]

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines only static variables in global scope: pkgname, pkgver, source, md5sums, etc. No command substitutions, eval, or other code execution occurs when the file is sourced. The `source` array specifies a git+https URL with a pinned tag, which is standard AUR practice. There is no `pkgver()` function. The only code that runs during `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` is the global variable assignments and the git clone, both safe. All suspicious operations (submodule update, cmake, make) are confined to `prepare()`, `build()`, and `package()`, which are not executed by the requested commands.</details>
<summary>No malicious code in global scope; only safe variable assignments and git clone.</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No malicious code in global scope; only safe variable assignments and git clone.[/]
